summary.intent: Fix a bug where the layered products scan pipeline triggered builds for images that referenced disabled dependencies (oadp-operator referencing disabled oadp-velero), causing build failures.
summary.outcome: Implemented a complete fix with disabled dependency detection in scan-sources report generation, filtering in both layered-products and ocp4 scan pipelines, improved error messaging in the rebaser, and comprehensive unit tests. Created a PR with all changes.

repo: The 'dependents' field in image YAML metadata creates reverse dependency links (e.g., oadp-velero.yml lists oadp-operator in dependents)
runtime.gitdata.load_data(path='images') loads ALL image YAML files regardless of enabled/disabled mode
Scan-sources reports use flags (okd_only, has_disabled_dependency) to communicate filtering metadata to pipelines
pyartcd pipelines follow a pattern of storing raw reports and filtering based on flags before triggering builds
Disabled images have 'mode: disabled' but wip images have 'mode: wip' and are not treated as disabled for dependency checks

code.finding: generate_report() is where scan-level filtering and flagging happens; uses runtime.gitdata to read all image metadata including disabled ones
_replace_internal_image_refs() resolves image references in operator CSVs; using late_resolve_image(required=False) returns None for disabled images instead of raising immediately
Pipelines use asyncio.gather with return_exceptions=True to handle per-image failures gracefully and collect failed_images
name_in_bundle_map is populated for ALL images (including disabled) during runtime initialization

workflow: This codebase uses local imports for exceptions (DoozerFatalError imported inside functions) rather than module-level imports
Tests need to be run from specific directories: pyartcd tests from pyartcd/, doozer tests from doozer/
ruff is used for both linting (--check) and auto-formatting (format)
Mock objects with spec=Runtime don't include dynamically-assigned attributes like gitdata; they must be set up explicitly

friction: Initial test failures because MagicMock(spec=Runtime) didn't recognize the dynamically-assigned gitdata attribute, requiring explicit mock setup
Tests needed to be run from different directories for doozer vs pyartcd components
Could not perform real dry-run verification because it requires Red Hat internal infrastructure (Brew, KUBECONFIG, ART Jenkins)
open_items: The fix is preventive (filters at scan time) but doesn't address the root cause if an operator's source code adds a reference to a disabled image after metadata is updated
No actual dry-run verification against production Jenkins infrastructure was performed; relying on unit test coverage
